title: BroMenu
html:
BroMenu is the original codename — and product spec — that became (or directly fed into) Brotein. The first explicit articulation is the late-2024 todos note (2024 12 21 todos), which contains the most structured product spec in the entire corpus. Yahir defines BroMenu as "a fitness-focused iOS mobile app for identifying the most anabolic menu items from restaurant menus, helping users achieve muscle building, bulking, or cutting goals." The stack is fully specced: SwiftUI frontend in WindSurf IDE, tested via Xcode on a physical iPhone (explicitly to avoid the Apple Developer fee), OpenAI Camera API for OCR plus GPT for anabolic scoring, and Supabase for storage and "Sign in with Apple" auth. The UI inspiration is named directly: "Mimic the sleek, real-time QR scanning experience of AliPay or WeChat, including animations and immediate feedback" — a direct callback to the China year.

The QA scope in that same note is unusually thorough for a solo founder pre-launch: camera permissions, throttling ("Slow down, bro!"), error scenarios (covered lens, blur, poor lighting), menu varieties (restaurant, cafe, handwritten, digital boards), Supabase integration, API quota, memory leaks via Instruments, battery drain, cellular data, interruption handling. Cost-consciousness (no runway discipline) bleeds into technical decisions: "Reduce API call costs by caching responses wherever possible."

By July 2025 the build-the-pipe-then-fill-it sequence is visible. 2025 07 09 screenshot 2025 07 09 at 4 51 29 pm 720cb8bf shows the live Supabase menu_items table with ~45 seeded rows (schema: id uuid, restaurant_id uuid, name text, description text). Eight days later, a 4 a.m. screenshot burst on July 17–18 captures corporate nutrition PDFs from Chipotle (2025 07 17 screenshot 2025 07 17 at 9 13 52 pm bb1a5662), Little Caesars (2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 2 41 11 am f76d9c99), Wingstop (2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 3 10 37 am fedfeec5), and Taco Bell (2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 13 am e3d1a9e8, 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 23 am 02657aa9) — Yahir scraping every chain he wanted anabolic scoring to cover. The seeded item names skew protein-heavy (Egg Bites, Protein Boxes, Egg White Wraps) — top-down by anabolic merit, not bottom-up by restaurant catalog.

The May 2025 todos (2025 05 30 todos) treat BroMenu/Brotein as the active venture: leaderboard, group share, TikTok marketing, "Brotein werun group." Whether BroMenu and Brotein are sibling concepts that converged or a rename remains an open question, but the lineage is unbroken.

Source summaries

  • 2024 12 21 todos — the canonical product spec
  • 2025 05 30 todos — operational state mid-2025
  • 2025 07 09 screenshot 2025 07 09 at 4 51 29 pm 720cb8bf — Supabase schema
  • 2025 07 17 screenshot 2025 07 17 at 9 13 52 pm bb1a5662 — Chipotle scrape
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 2 41 11 am f76d9c99 — Little Caesars scrape
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 3 10 37 am fedfeec5 — Wingstop scrape
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 13 am e3d1a9e8 — Taco Bell part 1
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 23 am 02657aa9 — Taco Bell part 2

Connections

  • brotein — the parent / successor product
  • anabolic scoring — the core mechanic
  • supabase — the backend
  • openai api — the AI layer
  • swiftui / xcode / windsurf ide — the build stack
  • fast food nutrition dataset — the seed data
  • restaurant menu scraping — the ingest pattern
  • alipay / wechat — the UI inspiration
  • no runway discipline — the design constraint
